response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack/web engineer with broad experience, but the role requires deep hardware-domain expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. Low-latency DLang services and brief C/C++ IDE work at National Instruments are the closest adjacencies, but they do not substitute for silicon performance architecture experience at the principal level.
strengths: 18+ years of software experience, satisfying the BS + 8 years qualification path
Proven low-latency systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware performance analysis experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or AI accelerator background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not silicon or systems architecture at principal level

key_factors.factor: No DNN workload or GPU analysis background
